diagrams.description: This timeline traces the historical evolution of Special Education from exclusion to inclusion. Key milestones: early pioneers like Jean‑Marc Itard (1801) and Edouard Seguin (1846) educating 'wild child' and children with intellectual disabilities; the era of institutionalization in the late 19th century; the mid‑20th century parent advocacy movement; Brown v. Board of Education (1954) laying groundwork against segregation; Section 504 of the Rehabilitation Act (1973) prohibiting discrimination; the groundbreaking court cases PARC v. Pennsylvania (1972) and Mills v. Board of Education (1972) establishing the right to education; the Education for All Handicapped Children Act (1975, later IDEA) mandating FAPE; IDEA reauthorizations (1990, 1997, 2004) strengthening provisions; ADA (1990); the inclusive education movement in the 1990s‑2000s; the UN Convention on the Rights of Persons with Disabilities (2006); and contemporary emphasis on UDL, RTI, and personalized learning.
